topics.label: Designing Data Processing Systems
topics.description: Covers selecting and configuring GCP storage and processing services to meet specific data requirements including latency, throughput, schema flexibility, and consistency guarantees. Includes Pub/Sub, Dataflow, Dataproc, Bigtable, BigQuery, Cloud Spanner, and Firestore, as well as architectural choices between streaming and batch processing.

examScenarios.title: Storage service selection
examScenarios.prompt: A scenario describes data volume, access patterns (point reads vs. analytical scans), latency requirements, and schema rigidity. You must identify which GCP storage service (Bigtable, Spanner, Firestore, Cloud SQL, BigQuery) is the best fit and explain why alternatives fall short.
examScenarios.focus: Matching workload characteristics to storage engine capabilities and trade-offs

examScenarios.title: Streaming pipeline design
examScenarios.prompt: A scenario requires processing Pub/Sub events with windowing, late data handling, or exactly-once semantics. You must select the correct Dataflow/Beam primitives (windowing strategy, triggers, allowed lateness, side inputs) and justify the design.
examScenarios.focus: Apache Beam concepts applied to real-time GCP data engineering scenarios

topics.label: Building and Operationalizing Data Pipelines
topics.description: Focuses on deploying, optimizing, monitoring, and maintaining production data pipelines on GCP. Covers BigQuery performance tuning (partitioning, clustering, slots, materialized views), Dataflow deployment and debugging, Cloud Composer DAG authoring and error handling, and CDC replication using Datastream.

examScenarios.title: BigQuery query cost and performance optimization
examScenarios.prompt: A query on a large table is slow or expensive. You are given details about the table schema, query predicates, and existing partitioning/clustering. You must identify the correct optimization (add partition pruning, cluster, use a materialized view, adjust slot reservations) and explain the trade-offs.
examScenarios.focus: BigQuery internals: how partitioning, clustering, and slot reservations affect query cost and speed

examScenarios.title: Pipeline failure and dead-letter handling
examScenarios.prompt: A streaming pipeline is experiencing repeated message failures or backlog growth. You must identify the root cause (hot key, quota exceeded, missing dead-letter config) and prescribe the correct remediation using Pub/Sub, Dataflow, or Cloud Monitoring features.
examScenarios.focus: Operationalizing streaming pipelines: failure modes, dead-letter queues, and backpressure management

topics.label: Operationalizing Machine Learning Models
topics.description: Covers the ML lifecycle on GCP including training with Vertex AI (AutoML, custom training, Vertex AI Pipelines), in-database ML with BigQuery ML, feature management with Vertex AI Feature Store, model deployment options (online vs. batch prediction), model monitoring for drift, and responsible AI practices.

examScenarios.title: Choosing an ML training approach
examScenarios.prompt: A scenario describes a business problem (classification, regression, forecasting), the team's ML expertise, and data location (BigQuery, Cloud Storage). You must choose the appropriate GCP ML approach (BigQuery ML, AutoML, custom training) and justify the selection based on effort, control, and scale.
examScenarios.focus: Trade-offs between no-code AutoML, SQL-based BigQuery ML, and custom training containers

examScenarios.title: Post-deployment model health
examScenarios.prompt: A deployed model's performance has degraded. You must identify whether the issue is prediction drift, data quality regression, or a serving infrastructure problem, and prescribe the correct Vertex AI monitoring or pipeline remediation.
examScenarios.focus: Model monitoring, drift detection, and the feature store's role in preventing training/serving skew

topics.label: Ensuring Solution Quality
topics.description: Addresses data governance, security, compliance, and quality assurance for data engineering solutions on GCP. Includes BigQuery access controls (column-level, row-level, authorized views), CMEK encryption, Cloud DLP for PII detection, Data Catalog and Dataplex for metadata management, data lineage, data quality validation, and cost optimization strategies.

examScenarios.title: Column and row-level access control
examScenarios.prompt: A scenario requires restricting access to specific columns (e.g., PII fields) or rows (e.g., date range, department) in a BigQuery table for different user groups, without duplicating data. You must identify the correct BigQuery security feature and configuration.
examScenarios.focus: BigQuery column-level security with policy tags vs. row-level security vs. authorized views

examScenarios.title: PII detection and governance pipeline
examScenarios.prompt: Raw data lands in Cloud Storage before being loaded into BigQuery. The compliance team requires automated PII scanning, tagging in the data catalog, and enforcement of encryption requirements. You must design the governance pipeline using Cloud DLP, Data Catalog, and Organization Policies.
examScenarios.focus: Integrating Cloud DLP, Data Catalog tagging, and CMEK enforcement in a data governance workflow

topics.label: Infrastructure and Pipeline Automation
topics.description: Covers automating GCP data infrastructure provisioning and pipeline deployment using Terraform, Cloud Build, Cloud Composer, Cloud Workflows, and GKE. Topics include Terraform state management and drift detection, CI/CD for Dataflow Flex Templates and DAGs, event-driven orchestration patterns, and containerizing data pipelines.

examScenarios.title: CI/CD for data pipelines
examScenarios.prompt: A team needs to promote a Dataflow pipeline or Cloud Composer DAG from development to production with automated testing and no manual steps. You must design the CI/CD workflow using Cloud Build, Artifact Registry, and appropriate promotion strategies.
examScenarios.focus: Cloud Build trigger configuration, Dataflow Flex Template build and push, and DAG versioning

examScenarios.title: Infrastructure as code for data workloads
examScenarios.prompt: A scenario describes a Terraform configuration for Dataproc, BigQuery, or Pub/Sub resources that produces unexpected plan output (drift, forced recreation, permission failure). You must identify the root cause and prescribe the correct Terraform fix.
examScenarios.focus: Terraform state, immutable fields, service account permissions, and resource dependency management on GCP
